name: Grand Challenges in Shape-Changing Interface Research
short_description: A CHI 2018 agenda paper that synthesizes 12 grand challenges to guide the maturing field of shape-changing interface research.
long_description: Authored by Alexander, Roudaut, Steimle, Hornbaek, Bruns Alonso, Follmer, and Merritt, this field-defining paper distills a workshop of 25 experts across design, computer science, HCI, engineering, robotics, and materials science into 12 grand challenges spanning technological, behavioural, and design concerns. Shape-changing interfaces use dynamic changes in a device's physical form as both input and output, making the body of the device itself a medium of interaction rather than a static enclosure for a screen. As a research agenda, it matters to physical AI and interaction design because it frames how dynamic physical embodiment, actuated materials, and morphing form factors should move from one-off prototypes toward coherent theory, evaluation, and ethics.
ai_note: This is an overview/agenda paper proposing research challenges; it contains no running AI/ML system, and shape-changing interfaces themselves are actuation and tangible-interaction techniques rather than AI.

images.caption: Figure from the CHI 2018 paper illustrating shape-changing interface research challenges.
images.alt_text: Diagram from an academic paper organizing grand challenges for shape-changing interface research across technological, behavioural, and design categories.
